title: hydrangea villosa var. velutina
intro: Hydrangea villosa var. velutina, also known as Mountain Hydrangea, is a deciduous shrub native to the mountainous regions of Asia, characterized by its large, round clusters of small white flowers. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The health benefits associated with Hydrangea villosa var. velutina include relief from arthritis, sore throats, and skin irritations, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. Hydrangea villosa var. velutina can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion for its medicinal uses, often in combination with other herbs to enhance its effects.
